name: S.T.A.S.I.S
subline: Stability Trends and Analysis for Shaping Investment Strategies
description: STASIS is a web application designed using Python, Javascript, and Django to allow for real-time and historical financial data to be provided from APIs and a MongoDB Database. It currently shows information for the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the S&P 500 that users can filter by Industry and Sector. In addition, STASIS also provides machine learning algorithms that can predict not only how volatile a security is but also how volatile it will be in the future.

name: T.O.W.E.R
subline: Thorough Offline Watchdog and Encrypted Reserve
description: TOWER is a cross-platform application designed in C++ and Qt that specialized in data protection. It alllows for you to safely store passwords and files and uses RSA Encryption techniques to store them. It uses an SQLite database as well as other encryption algorithms to ensure protection, as well as Google's 2FA Authentication API. It is able to monitor internet traffic in order to detect potentially suspicious access.

name: Crypto
description: Recently I had become interested in cryptograms and puzzles. In that spirit, I decided to design an IOS application that could decrypt them, as well as allow people to make their own cryptograms and generate secure passwords. Cryptograms can be entered by hand or use Apple's AI Vision Kit to extract text from images, though results depend on the image quality. My decryption approach utilized natural language processing, as it focused on analyzing finidng the best words rather than individual letters. This approach allows for a long cryptogram to be solved in seconds in contrast to a brute force approach.

name: Syntext
subline: Package-less Neural Network designed in Julia
description: As part of my Programming Languages course, I designed an Artificial Neural Network purely in Julia with the intention to classify text into different genres. As my goal was to learn the language syntax, I made an effort not to use any packages and design each part of the network myself. This project not only allowed me to learn more about machine learning, but also showed the strength of Julia as a programming language, which is just as useful as Python with the benefit of faster runtimes.

name: RecycleFinder
description: This was a group effort as part of my Software Engineering course, along with Michael Dadi, Victor Zapata III, and Federico . We designed an Android application that provided users with recycling related information. The Google Maps API and Google Firebase API were used to store information on recycling spots near the user, and the NewsAPI was used to provide articles relevant to sustainability.

name: Outbreak
subline: Reinforcement Learning Game and Programming Exercise
description: As part of my time as a Co-Instructor for the Serious Games and Artificial Intelligence course at the MIT Lincoln Laboratory, I designed a simple game that can be navigated by a reinforcement learning agent. The objective is to keep your citizens alive during a zombie outbreak, with the ability to move them around a board to protect them. Students were then given the project to make their own.
